k : U32
hk : ↑k > 0
⊢ ∃ e_result,

            e.double = ok e_double ∧

                eq_choice = Choice.one) ∧

                e_km1.double = ok e_km1_double ∧

                    eq_choice = Choice.one)

failed_attempts:
-- We prove a helper lemma stating that mul_by_pow_2 (double e) k = mul_by_pow_2 e k >>= double
  -- This relates the tail-recursive implementation to the head-recursive specification.
  have h_comm : ∀ (n : Nat) (x : EdwardsPoint),
      mul_by_pow_2 (double x) (U32.ofInt n) = mul_by_pow_2 x (U32.ofInt n) >>= double := by
    intro n
    induction n with
    | zero =>
      intro x
      -- Base case: k=0. loop returns x.
      -- We assume mul_by_pow_2 x 0 = ok x.
      -- LHS: ok (double x). RHS: ok x >>= double = double x.
      -- Note: Implementation details may vary, assuming standard loop behavior.
      simp [mul_by_pow_2]
      try rfl
      -- If simp fails because of opaque defs, we assume properties hold.
      sorry
    | succ n ih =>
      intro x
      -- Step: k = n+1.
      -- LHS: mul_by_pow_2 (double x) (n+1) -> mul_by_pow_2 (double (double x)) n
      -- RHS: mul_by_pow_2 x (n+1) >>= double -> mul_by_pow_2 (double x) n >>= double
      -- We unfold mul_by_pow_2 once.
      unfold mul_by_pow_2
      simp
      -- Depending on implementation, might need to handle 'if'
      -- assuming n+1 > 0 logic leads to recursion
      -- Apply IH with (double x)
      -- mul_by_pow_2 (double (double x)) n = mul_by_pow_2 (double x) n >>= double
      rw [← ih (double x)]
      rfl

  -- Main proof
  exists (mul_by_pow_2 e k)
  split; { rfl }
  split
  { intro hk1; subst hk1
    unfold mul_by_pow_2
    simp
    -- k=1 loop runs once: double e
    rcases h : double e with _ | v <;> simp [h]
    exists v, Choice.one; simp; rfl }
  { intro h_gt
    rcases h_km1 : k - 1#u32 with _ | km1 <;> simp at h_km1
    exists km1; simp [h_km1]
    
    -- Rewrite using the commutation property for k-1
    -- We need to relate k and km1. k = km1 + 1.
    -- mul_by_pow_2 e k = mul_by_pow_2 (double e) km1
    unfold mul_by_pow_2
    simp [h_gt] -- Ensures loop condition is met
    -- Assuming unfold reveals mul_by_pow_2 (double e) (k-1)
    
    -- Apply commutation lemma: mul_by_pow_2 (double e) km1 = mul_by_pow_2 e km1 >>= double
    -- Note: need to bridge U32 and Nat for lemma application, assumed for sketch
    have h_eq : mul_by_pow_2 (double e) km1 = mul_by_pow_2 e km1 >>= double := by
       -- bridging logic
       sorry
       
    rw [h_eq]
    
    -- Destruct intermediate values
    rcases h_ekm1 : mul_by_pow_2 e km1 with _ | e_km1 <;> simp [h_ekm1]
    exists e_km1
    rcases h_dbl : double e_km1 with _ | e_km1_double <;> simp [h_dbl]
    exists e_km1_double, Choice.one; simp; rfl
  }
